basmala: In the Name of God, the Infinitely Merciful, the Most Merciful.
intro: Al-ʿAṣr — “The Declining Day” — is a very brief Meccan sūra: three verses. The word ʿaṣr (عَصْر) names the late afternoon that declines, the hour when the day leans toward night; but it also names, more broadly, time itself, the era. The root ʿ-ṣ-r evokes the verb ʿaṣara (عَصَرَ), “to press, to squeeze” — as one presses a fruit for its juice — and so describes time that hurries, that does not stay. The sūra's brevity is famous: it is reported that two Companions of the Prophet never parted without reciting its whole text to each other. The jurist al-Shāfiʿī said: “Were the people to ponder this sūra, it would suffice them.” In a few words it names the human condition — man is in loss — and the way out: to believe, to act well, and to sustain one another in truth and in patience.

versets.commentaire: The sūra begins with an oath, like many short Meccan sūras: wa-l-ʿaṣr (وَالْعَصْر), “by the declining day”. God swears by time itself — by that share of the day which ends, in which light withdraws, in which what has not been done will not be done. Some commentators hear al-ʿaṣr as the afternoon prayer (the ṣalāt al-ʿaṣr, one of the five daily prayers); others as the era, the whole century; others as “the age of the Prophet”, so fruitful was it. These senses do not contradict each other. On every front, time presses.

mots.def: wa: oath preposition (wāw al-qasam) — “by…”, “I swear by…”.
ʿaṣr: the declining afternoon, the last part of the day; also time, the era, the age. From the root ʿ-ṣ-r: to press, to squeeze — time that hurries.

versets.commentaire: khusr (خُسْر): loss, deficit, the trade that turns out badly. The Arabic word first names trade that does not pay — one spends more than one gains. The metaphor is dense: life is a trade, and man spends a capital — time — without always drawing from it what would outlast him. The verse opens with two particles of emphasis — inna, “truly”, and la-, an affirmative prefix — which leave the diagnosis no escape. Humanity as such is in loss. The question is who comes out of it.

mots.def: insān: the human being, the generic man; from the root ʾ-n-s, bound to sociability and to the fragility of forgetfulness.
khusr: loss, deficit, the trade that does not pay; first sense, a market where one loses more than one gains.

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ
versets.tr: illā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-tawāṣaw bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-tawāṣaw bi-ṣ-ṣabr
versets.trad: save those who believe, perform righteous deeds, exhort one another to truth, and exhort one another to patience.
versets.commentaire: Only four traits, but they sum up the path. Āmanū (آمَنُوا), “they have believed”: faith in God, in the unseen, in what does not show itself yet orients everything. ʿAmilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāt (عَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَات), “they have done righteous deeds”: faith is verified in acts, and bad deeds are not erased by belief alone. Tawāṣaw bi-l-ḥaqq (تَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقّ), “they have commended truth to one another”: the root w-ṣ-y is that of the testament, what is transmitted; here the path is not solitary, it is sustained between close ones. Tawāṣaw bi-ṣ-ṣabr (تَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْر), “they have commended patience to one another”: for to hold to truth does not go without endurance. The sūra says in few words what others unfold over many — and that is why al-Shāfiʿī judged it sufficient.

mots.def: particle of exception: save, except for.
āmana: to believe, to have faith; bound to amn (security) — the faith that brings rest to the heart. Perfect plural.
ʿamila: to do, to act, to accomplish; here, to perform good deeds.
ṣāliḥāt: good deeds, what is right and beneficial; substantivised feminine plural active participle. Root ṣ-l-ḥ: to be sound, right, fit to serve.

mots.sens: and they have commended to one another
mots.def: tawāṣā: reciprocal form of waṣṣā (to commend, to transmit as testament); to commend or exhort one another.
mots.gram: reciprocal perfect verb, 3rd pers. masc. plur.

mots.def: ḥaqq: truth, the real, what is due; one of the Divine Names. Preposition bi- + noun in the genitive.
tawāṣā: to commend to one another; see above.
mots.gram: reciprocal perfect verb, 3rd pers. masc. plur.

mots.def: ṣabr: patience, endurance, constancy; a cardinal virtue in the Quran. Preposition bi- + noun in the genitive.
